title: asplenium seelosii subsp. celtibericum
intro: Asplenium seelosii subsp. celtibericum, also known as the Iberian spleenwort, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Asplenium seelosii subsp. celtibericum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve antioxidant defenses, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in some regions, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its commercial availability is limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
